title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Mochizuki is a highly talented detective at the Agency for Faith, renowned for his ability to logically process intelligent crimes. He maintains a close friendship with Inspector Jinguuji of the Metropolitan Police Department. Although his reputation as a "genius" previously brought him a massive volume of private commissions, he has recently begun rejecting most of them, choosing to accept only cases that genuinely interest him rather than trivial disputes among the upper class.

The narrator, Hagio—a mystery novelist and long-time acquaintance of Mochizuki since their university days—visits him and observes a peculiar letter. The letter is written on high-quality, light yellow stationery featuring a floral pattern. The handwriting is light and appears to have been written quickly with a fountain pen. The content is brief and high-handed, consisting of a single sentence: "Come to my residence at 10:00 a.m. on December 2nd for consultation."

The sender is identified as Tomijiro Itô, a name associated with the Ito Group, a massive corporation that evolved from the Meiji-era Ito Silk Factory into a large zaibatsu currently represented by Kinmiri Heavy Industry. The letter is linked to the mysterious Kinmiri family and their reclusive lifestyle at "Crow Castle," a legendary and secretive mansion in Kyoto. Hagio suspects the letter might be related to the recent death of Mukayama Tadashi, the chairman of Mukayama Seisakusho. Mochizuki expresses interest in the letter, suggesting it is significant.
